title: Customer Success Manager / Loan Processor
description:
About FINR Advisory

Watch our company video here: 
/share/22f7b670ce4b4775a2eaf40fe652ffcf



FINR Advisory is a boutique Finance Broking firm offering Residential, Commercial and Asset Finance Broking services to investors who are looking to grow their portfolios or developers looking for their next development deal.



We are looking for a friendly and enthusiastic Customer Success Manager/ Loan Processor to join our growing team based out of Melbourne, Victoria, Australia. Working alongside the Broker, Para-broker and the extended team, the main focus for this role will be to conduct credit assessments on a potential client’s personal and financial situation.



**Please don't apply if you have less than 2 years of experience in the Australian Mortgage Industry**



The Role

• General administration duties including handling the office phone and office

• Actively managing client relationships, collecting additional supporting documentation, and providing loan status updates.

• Manage conditionally approved applications and address lender information requests.

• Communicate with key stakeholders including lenders, real estate agents, solicitors and accountants.

• Liaising with clients to provide further information and to chase up documentation

• Provide administrative scheduling and communication support to executive team.

• Other special projects, administrative or operations related duties as assigned.

• Reviewing client Fact find and supporting information provided by the client to ensure we have all the information required to complete preliminary assessments.

• Completing lender pricing approvals, client credit checks and ordering valuations

• Loan processing which includes data input into Aggregator CRM – Mercury and ApplyOnline, Lender document collection and packaging for submission

• Assisting the Finance Broker to ensure that all compliance requirements are met



About you

• Minimum 2 years experience in the Australian mortgage broking/banking industry as a credit assessor/analyst or loan processor

• Experience with the Connective Mercury Platform and ApplyOnline is desirable but not essential.

• Experience with the CRM Broker Engine or Sales Trekker is desirable but not essential.

• High attention to detail and extremely organised

• Great at managing workload and prioritising tasks where necessary to meet deadlines.

• Excellent English comprehension and fluency with good attention to grammar and spelling can-do attitude with the ability to work autonomously in a fast-paced environment

• Be willing to work collaboratively and be driven as a team player



Home office requirements:

• Reliable, high speed internet connection (10MB or greater)

• Secondary backup internet connection (hotspot or comparable)

• Reliable and up to date personal computer with minimum 8GB RA
mory capacity to run various programs and applications.



Compensation:

• Php 50,000.00 – Php 70,000.00 (Depending on experience and skill set).

• Rewarding Team bonus shared equally amongst all staff (local/offshore)

• Paid Philippine Regular Holidays

• 13th Month Pay

• Strong Career Growth opportunities

• New hires will go through a 90-day probationary evaluation period.



APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS:

**Please follow the steps below carefully. Applications that do not meet the requirements will be automatically declined.**



To submit your application for the job, please complete all of the following steps and respond to the job add or send an
 to 
 with the subject line "I read the whole ad - (Your Name)"



To apply, please include ALL of the following:

1. Please create a 2-3 minute audio recording or video explaining why you would be a good fit for this position and why you are the best candidate. Please state the date and time of your recording in the video.

2. Perform an Internet speed test at 
/ by clicking the “start” button in the middle of their homepage. After completion of the test, take a screenshot of your results and
 it as a JPEG.

3. Include a link to your onlinejobs.ph profile and attach your CV.



The application process is designed to ensure you follow directions and pay attention to detail.



Looking forward to meeting you!

title: Senior Sylius / Symfony Developer
description:
About Us



We are building an innovative, upcoming online marketplace. We are moving away from traditional auction models to a dual-sales approach: "Buy It Now" and "Make an Offer."



We operate a full-service consignment model where we handle the physical inventory, inspecting, photographing, and storing the goods, while providing a seamless digital experience for both buyers and sellers. Our platform will integrate advanced features like a digital wallet for sale proceeds, hybrid payments, complex shipping logistics (including "Hold in Cellar"), and multi-channel sales via Google Shopping, Amazon Marketplace and eBay.



We are a small, experienced founding team looking for a highly skilled technical partner to build the core platform.



The Role



We are seeking a Senior Sylius / Symfony Developer to architect and build the platform from the ground up. You will be responsible for taking our detailed technical specifications and turning them into a robust, scalable, and secure e-commerce marketplace.



This is not a standard e-commerce build. You will be developing complex custom modules on top of the Sylius framework to handle our unique business logic.



Key Responsibilities



• Platform Architecture: Design and implement the core platform using Sylius and Symfony.

• Custom Module Development: Build sophisticated custom modules, including:



• Offer Engine: A state machine-driven system allowing buyers to submit offers and sellers to accept/reject them.

• Wallet & Hybrid Payments: A digital wallet system for sellers to hold proceeds, integrated with Stripe Connect for complex payouts and hybrid (wallet + card) checkout flows.

• Warehousing & Logistics: A backend module to manage physical inventory, barcode generation, storage locations, and consolidated shipping ("Hold in Cellar").

• Multi-Channel Integrations: Automated feeds for Google Shopping and a comprehensive two-way integration with the eBay API.

• API Development: Design and build RESTful APIs to support a future mobile application.

• Third-Party Integrations: Integrate with Stripe Connect, shipping providers, and potentially external identity/age verification services.

• Quality Assurance: Write comprehensive unit and integration tests to ensure platform stability and security.



Required Skills & Experience



• Deep Sylius Expertise: Proven experience building complex, custom e-commerce platforms using Sylius. You must understand how to extend Sylius core components, state machines, and resource management.

• Symfony Mastery: Strong background in PHP 8+ and the Symfony framework.

• Marketplace Experience: Previous experience building multi-vendor marketplaces or platforms with complex payment splitting (Stripe Connect experience is highly desirable).

• API Design: Experience designing and consuming RESTful APIs.

• Database Design: Strong SQL skills and experience with Doctrine ORM.

• Frontend Integration: While this is primarily a backend role, you should be comfortable working with Twig templates and integrating frontend assets.

• Testing: Commitment to automated testing (PHPUnit, Behat).



Work Setup



• Flexible hours: We are outcome-focused. Delivery matters more than specific hours logged.

• Collaboration: You will work directly with the founders and our UX/UI design team.



Application Questions (Required)



If this sounds like the challenge you've been looking for, please apply with your CV/LinkedIn, links to relevant GitHub repositories or live projects, and answers to the following questions:



1. Sylius Experience: Describe the most complex custom module or feature you have built on top of Sylius. What was the business problem, and how did you solve it technically?

2. Marketplace Payments: Have you worked with Stripe Connect or similar complex payment splitting systems? If so, briefly describe the implementation.

3. State Machines: Our "Make an Offer" feature requires a robust state machine (Pending -> Accepted/Rejected/Expired). How would you approach implementing this within the Sylius ecosystem?

4. MVP Delivery: If you had to deliver the core MVP (Buy It Now + Make an Offer + Basic Stripe integration) in 8-10 weeks, what would be your technical approach and what potential bottlenecks would you anticipate?

5. Availability & Rates: What is your general availability each week, and what is your expected salary?

6. What excites you most about joining a startup team building something new from the ground up?



Please also let us know why you are the right person, explain your experience and tell us why we should hire you.

title: Senior Medusa.js Developer
description:
About USs

We are building a next-generation, premium marketplace. We are moving beyond the traditional "Buy It Now" model to create a dynamic trading platform that includes a sophisticated "Make an Offer" negotiation engine, a digital wallet system, and a full-service physical consignment and warehousing operation.



This is not a standard e-commerce build. We are creating a complex, multi-sided marketplace with unique business logic, hybrid payment flows, and physical inventory management. We have chosen Medusa.js as our foundational architecture because we need total headless flexibility, robust custom workflows, and native marketplace capabilities.



The Role



We are seeking a Senior Medusa.js Developer to lead the technical architecture and development of the platform. You will be responsible for building the core custom modules that differentiate our business, integrating with third-party services, and ensuring the platform is scalable, secure, and performant.



You will work closely with the founding team to translate complex business requirements into elegant, maintainable Node.js/TypeScript code.



Key Responsibilities & Custom Modules to Build



1. The Offer Engine (State Machine): Build a robust negotiation system allowing buyers to submit offers, sellers to accept/decline/counter, and manage the complex state transitions and time-expirations of these offers using Medusa's Workflows SDK.



2. Digital Wallet & Hybrid Payments: Implement a user wallet system where users can hold funds from sales. Crucially, you must build a custom checkout flow that allows a single transaction to be split between wallet funds and a credit card (via Stripe Connect).



3. Warehousing & Consignment Module: Develop a custom backend module to manage physical inventory. This includes generating unique barcode IDs for incoming stock, tracking storage locations, and managing the "Hold in" logic (where buyers purchase but delay shipping to consolidate orders).



4. Stripe Connect Integration: Architect the complex payment splitting and automated payouts required for a multi-vendor marketplace, ensuring compliance and seamless onboarding for our consignors.



5. Multi-Channel Sync: Build robust integrations to sync our "Buy It Now" inventory with Google Shopping and eBay, ensuring real-time stock updates across all channels.



Required Skills & Experience



• Deep Medusa.js Expertise: Proven experience building complex, custom e-commerce solutions using Medusa.js (v1 or v2). You must understand its core architecture, plugin system, and Workflows SDK.



• Node.js & TypeScript: Expert-level proficiency in backend JavaScript/TypeScript development.



• Marketplace Architecture: Experience building multi-vendor marketplaces, handling split payments, and managing complex user roles.



• Stripe Connect: Deep understanding of Stripe Connect (Custom or Express accounts) for marketplace routing and payouts.



• API Design: Strong experience designing and consuming RESTful and GraphQL APIs.



• Database Management: Proficiency with PostgreSQL and Redis.



Work Setup



• Flexible hours / outcome-focused delivery: We care about what you build, not when you log on.



• Experienced Team: Work with a team running multiple SaaS brands and an established digital agency.



To Apply



Please submit:



1. Your CV or LinkedIn/GitHub profile.



2. Links to any relevant projects, demos, or code (specifically Medusa.js projects if possible).



3. Your answers to the following application questions.



Application Questions (Required)



If the above sounds like you, please apply. Your application will include the following questions:



1. What interests you most about building a complex, multi-sided marketplace?



2. Describe a previous project where you built a custom e-commerce solution or marketplace using Medusa.js. What was the most complex custom module you had to build, and how did you approach it?



3. We require a hybrid payment system where a user can pay for a $1,000 order using $400 from their digital wallet and $600 via credit card. How would you architect this custom checkout flow within Medusa.js and Stripe?



4. We need a robust "Make an Offer" engine with states like Pending, Accepted, Countered, and Expired. How would you leverage Medusa's Workflows SDK or custom services to build this state machine reliably?



5. If you had to deliver an MVP of this platform in 8–10 weeks, what would you prioritise, what would you defer, and what specific Medusa plugins or starters would you leverage to accelerate development?



6. What is your general availability each week?



7. What excites you most about joining a startup team building something new from the ground up?



8. What is your expected salary expectation?



9. Please also let us know why you are the right person, explain your experience, and tell us why we should hire you.

title: Media Buyer - FB
description:
Joburn Pte. Ltd.



We're an AI-powered growth agency. We've driven over $5M in client revenue in two years — our flagship client went from $15K/month to over $1M ARR under our system.



We don't do "run some ads and see what happens." We build compounding growth machines: diagnostic audits, analytics pipelines, ad creative engines, CRM automation, and conversion tracking — all wired together so every dollar spent teaches us something.



The agency is scaling from a handful of high-touch clients to 50+ Growth Partner accounts. That's where you come in.



What you're actually doing



You're the person who makes sure campaigns are built right, running clean, and improving every week.



Here's what a typical day looks like:



Morning: Check in via Slack standup. Review yesterday's ad performance across client accounts in Meta Ads Manager. Flag anything bleeding money.

Mid-day: Launch new ad sets, swap out fatigued creatives, adjust budgets based on CPA and ROAS trends. Most of this takes 30–60 minutes per client — not days.

Afternoon: Pull performance data, build client reports that actually say something useful (not just screenshots of dashboards), and feed winning patterns back into our system so the next round of creative is sharper than the last.

You'll work primarily inside Meta Ads Manager 
 + Instagram), with more platforms coming as we scale. You'll also touch GoHighLevel (our CRM — tracking leads from ad click to closed deal), 
 (automation that routes creatives, pulls data, and syncs everything), and Supabase (where all the performance data lives).



You don't need to be an expert in all of those on day one. But you need to be fluent in Ads Manager. That's non-negotiable.



The AI part — read this carefully



Our system uses AI for roughly 90–95% of the operational work. Claude writes first-draft ad copy. 
 routes it through approval flows. HeyGen and ElevenLabs produce video creative. Supabase stores and surfaces the analytics.



Your job is the final 5–10%. The part that actually determines whether the money was well spent. You're checking that campaign structures make sense, audiences are right, budgets aren't misconfigured, and nothing's quietly wasting spend at 2am.



That 5–10% is where the skill lives. AI can generate. You d
 what ships.



And here's the part most job posts won't say: at some point, AI will handle media buying end-to-end. The people who thrive here are the ones who treat AI as a tool that makes them faster — not a threat they're competing against. If you understand that and want to be on the right side of it, we want to talk.



What we need from you



Must-haves:



Hands-on experience in
 Ads Manager. You've spent real budgets, managed real campaigns, and made real optimization decisions. Not "I completed a course." You should be able to open an unfamiliar ad account, audit what's running, and tell us what's broken within 30 minutes.

You think in hours, not days. When something needs doing, you do it now. Most tasks here are 30–60 minutes, maybe two hours. "I'll get to it by end of week" doesn't work here.

Analytical instinct. You see a CPL spike and you already have three hypotheses before anyone asks.

Detail obsession. Wrong UTM parameters, mismatched audiences, typos in ad copy — that stuff should physically bother you.

Rock-solid internet. Not "usually fine." You're managing live ad spend. A dropped connection at the wrong time costs real money.

Good to have:



Experience with GoHighLevel, 
, or any CRM/automation platform

Comfort working alongside AI tools (if you already use ChatGPT or Claude for work, say so)

Experience building client-facing performance reports — not just pulling numbers, but explaining what they mean and what to do next

What you get



Hourly role. You start at a rate that reflects where you are right now. Prove you can execute, and the rate moves up. We don't cap people who deliver results.

Performance incentives tied to the outcomes you drive. We reward people who make our clients money.

Training on our systems. You'll learn our AI stack, our creative frameworks, our reporting standards. We invest in people who are in it for real — but we can't teach you how to think. The foundations need to already be there.

A seat at a small, fast team. This isn't a 200-person agency where you're a cog. You'll work directly with the people building the system. Your input shapes how we operate.

100% remote. Work from wherever. Just be reachable, be fast, and deliver.

To apply



Reply with the subject line: "The capital of Japan is [your answer]"



If you can't follow that one instruction, we already have our answer.

title: Senior Video Editor (Ai tools Expert) (Performance Marketing – DTC)
description:
Company: Lemon Adds

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Type: Full-Time

Hours: PST overlap required



About Lemon Adds:

Lemon Adds is a performance-driven eCommerce marketing agency scaling DTC brands to 7 and 8 figures.

We operate at the intersection of paid media, creative strategy, and AI-driven production.

Our focus is simple: content that drives measurable revenue.



The Role

We are hiring an elite-level video editor focused on performance, not aesthetics.

This role combines direct response thinking, UGC-style execution, and AI-powered production. Your work will directly impact ad performance, conversion rates, and revenue across multiple DTC brands.



What You’ll Do

- Produce high-converting video ads for Meta, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and X, with a strong focus on hooks, pacing, and conversion-driven structure.

- Edit and repurpose UGC and influencer-style content into performance creatives that feel native to each platform and are designed to convert.

- Use AI tools such as Runway, Pika, Descript, and ElevenLabs to scale production, including generating voiceovers, UGC-style content, and synthetic assets.

Work hands-on with Stable Diffusion (ComfyUI / A1111 or similar) to generate custom visual assets and integrate them into ad creatives.

- Translate creative briefs into direct-response videos using proven frameworks (hook ? problem ? solution ? proof ? CTA), and collaborate closely with media buyers and strategists.

- Continuously iterate based on performance data, producing multiple variations and identifying winning patterns.

-Help improve internal workflows by contributing to scalable editing and AI production systems.



Requirements:

*4–6+ years of professional video editing experience, with a strong focus on DTC or performance marketing.

*Proven ability to create high-converting ad creatives. Portfolio must reflect performance-driven work, not just visual quality.

*Strong experience with UGC and influencer-style editing, including raw footage, testimonial formats, and native-looking ads.

*Advanced proficiency in Premiere Pro and After Effects (or equivalent), with a deep understanding of short-form, mobile-first content.

*Hands-on experience with AI tools for video, voice, and content generation, and the ability to integrate them into production workflows.

*Practical experience with Stable Diffusion and tools like ComfyUI or A1111, including prompt control and asset generation.

*Strong understanding of direct response marketing, including hooks, retention, and conversion mechanics.

*Fluent English, proactive communication, and ability to work fully aligned with PST hours.



What Success Looks Like:

*Consistent output of high-performing creatives that drive measurable results.

*Ability to increase production volume without sacrificing performance.

*Contribution to scalable AI-driven creative systems within the team.

*Clear impact on campaign performance and client revenue.



Why Lemon Adds?

You’ll work with fast-scaling DTC brands and be part of a team that prioritizes results over fluff.

This is a high-ownership role with real growth potential, especially for someone who wants to operate at the forefront of AI and performance marketing.



How to Apply: 

Submit your portfolio with relevant performance ads, including UGC-style work and AI-generated content to 

Include a short Loom video walking through 1–2 ads you created and explaining why they performed.

s that feel human; use AI tools for drafting while ensuring final output is not robotic.

ntum.

title: Level 2 MSP Technician – Microsoft 365 / Intune / Security (Remote WFH)
description:
## Level 2 MSP Technician – Microsoft 365 / Intune / Security (Remote WFH)  

**Tel IT Solutions | Work from Home (Remote) | Adelaide / South Australia business hours (ACST/ACDT) | Full-time**



Tel IT Solutions is a managed IT services and cybersecurity provider supporting Australian businesses. We’re hiring an experienced **Level 2 technician** with strong Microsoft 365 + Intune capability who can deliver reliable remote support, modern device deployments, and security uplift across multiple client environments.



### Minimum experience requirement (must meet)

- **5+ years’ experience in Microsoft 365 / Intune** plus **Level 2 desktop/systems support** (or equivalent)



### What you’ll be doing

- Provide **Level 2 remote support** (ticket-based) across Microsoft 365, Windows, endpoint, and core business systems

- Administer **Microsoft 365**: Entra ID (Azure AD), Exchange Online, SharePoint/OneDrive, Teams, licensing, groups, policies

- Manage **Microsoft Intune**: configuration profiles, compliance policies, app deployment, update rings, reporting

- Build and maintain **Windows Autopilot / zero-touch deployments** and standardised device build processes

- Implement and manage security controls: **MFA, Conditional Access, Microsoft Defender**, security baselines, hardening and best practice

- Troubleshoot identity/access issues, mail flow, Teams issues, SharePoint permissions, OneDrive sync, device compliance/enrolment

- Assist with onboarding/offboarding, endpoint refreshes, M365 uplift projects, and security improvements

- Produce clear documentation (SOPs, KB articles) and contribute to improving internal processes



### Must-have skills

- **5+ years** hands-on **Microsoft 365 + Intune** experience

- Strong **Level 2 support** skills (Windows 10/11, troubleshooting, escalation, customer communication)

- Good understanding of **identity and access** (Entra ID, MFA, Conditional Access concepts)

- Experience with **Autopilot** and automated deployments

- Solid security fundamentals (least privilege, patching, endpoint protection)

- Strong documentation habits and ability to work independently from home



### Nice-to-have (highly regarded)

- Microsoft Defender for Endpoint / Defender for Office 365

- PowerShell scripting/automation for M365/Entra/Exchange

- M365 migrations (mailboxes, SharePoint/OneDrive)

- Networking/firewalls/VPN exposure (FortiGate/Sophos/Meraki)

- Microsoft certifications: **MS-102, MD-102, AZ-104** (or equivalent)



### Work setup (important)

- **100% remote / work from home**

- Must reliably work **Adelaide / South Australia business hours (ACST/ACDT)**

- Stable internet + professional home office setup



### How to apply

Send your CV plus a short note covering:

1) Your years of experience with **Microsoft 365 + Intune**  

2) Your strongest **Autopilot/Intune deployment** example  

3) A recent **security improvement** you implemented

title: Client Success Specialist (AU Clients, Remote, Forever) | Advance Global Medical Research
description:
CLIENT SUCCESS SPECIALIST WANTED: SHAPE THE FUTURE OF MEDICAL RESEARCH



-100% remote, forever

-Unlimited paid leave



Hello! We’re Trialfacts, a remote, entrepreneurial company at the forefront of advancing health research. We’re looking for a CLIENT SUCCESS SPECIALIST to help our clients successfully recruit participants for their clinical trials. If you’re a relationship-builder with strong organizational and analytical skills, and you thrive in a proactive, forward-thinking, and fast-paced remote environment, this role is for you.



YOUR ROLE:



CLIENT ONBOARDING & SUPPORT: Guide new clients through our onboarding process, ensuring they feel confident using our systems and understand how we help them reach their goals.

ENROLLMENT SUCCESS: Take initiative to proactively monitor client projects, develop strategies to ensure enrollment targets are met, and swiftly address any roadblocks along the way.

CLIENT RETENTION AND REFERRALS: Build strong relationships with clients by delivering anticipatory and exceptional service that encourages repeat business and enthusiastic referrals.

COMMUNICATION: Maintain clear, timely, and proactive communication that not only keeps clients informed but also anticipates their needs  and ensures projects stay on track.

QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE: Ensure quality standards are met and client and participant data are handled securely, in line with privacy regulations.



WHAT WE OFFER:



AUTONOMY & FLEXIBILITY: Set your own hours depending on what works best for our clients, enjoy unlimited paid holidays, and work from anywhere with a good internet connection.

INNOVATION & IMPACT: Work with research teams driving significant advancements in medical research from organizations like AstraZeneca, Harvard, Stanford, and many more.

GROWTH AND LEARNING: Opportunities for professional development, with access to courses and training programs.

REWARDING CAREER: Contribute meaningfully to the world of health research and share in the success of Trialfacts.



THIS POSITION IS IDEAL FOR YOU IF YOU:



ARE CLIENT FOCUSED: You thrive on understanding client needs and go the extra mile to anticipate and address them early.

EXCEL AT BUILDING RELATIONSHIPS: You naturally develop trust and rapport with clients, encouraging repeat business and referrals.

ARE A PROACTIVE PROBLEM SOLVER: You tackle challenges head-on, always looking for solutions that keep client projects moving forward and prevent issues before they arise.

ARE A STRATEGIC THINKER: You can review data and information, conduct root cause analyses, and resolve problems with mitigative and corrective strategies.

COMMUNICATE CLEARLY AND EFFECTIVELY: You’re skilled in both written and verbal communication, adapting your style to suit different audiences.

ARE ORGANISED AND DETAIL-ORIENTED: You juggle multiple priorities with ease, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks.

UNDERSTAND THE IMPORTANT OF DATA PRIVACY: You’re conscientious about handling sensitive information and complying with privacy standards.



INTERESTED IN JOINING US?

If this position aligns with your career aspirations and skills, we would love to hear from you. As part of our selection process, the final 1 to 3 candidates will undertake a paid trial for 1–2 weeks. This trial period is a vital step in our recruitment process and candidates will be compensated for their time and effort.



Apply here:

title: Test Analyst - Remote
description:
We are seeking a detail-focused Test Analyst to support the quality and reliability of our core business systems. In this role, you will help ensure that enterprise applications function as expected by applying structured testing approaches across multiple stages of the development lifecycle.



The ideal candidate brings a solid IT foundation, strong knowledge of both the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and software testing lifecycle (STLC), and the ability to analyze systems thoroughly to identify potential issues before release.



Key Responsibilities:

- Test Planning and Preparation

- Collaborate with business analysts and developers to define testing strategies

- Develop detailed test plans and test cases for new implementations and system enhancements

- Test Execution and Defect Management

- Perform testing across multiple phases including system, integration, regression, and UAT

- Record and track defects, ensuring issues are resolved before release

- Provide regular updates on testing progress and results

- Enterprise System Validation

- Perform thorough testing of ERP systems and related business workflows

- Integration and Data Testing

- Verify data exchanges and message accuracy when testing EDI transactions and API endpoints

- Database Validation

- Use SQL queries to validate backend data and confirm database accuracy

- Continuous Improvement

- Recommend improvements to testing practices, coverage, and overall efficiency



Required Qualifications:

- Minimum 3 years of experience in software testing

- ISTQB Foundation Level certification or higher

- Strong knowledge of system, integration, and regression testing approaches

- Experience testing ERP platforms and EDI integrations/message formats

- Practical experience performing API testing

- Ability to write and execute SQL queries for backend validation

- Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills



Preferred Experience:

- Experience conducting performance testing, including load, stress, or volume testing

- Experience creating and maintaining test automation scripts using Java



Work Schedule :

6:30 AM - 3:00 PM Philippines Time

title: In Need of a Senior US Tax Preparer
description:
I need a tax preparer job description. Here are the details:



*Bridged Financial Solutions — boutique remote accounting and advisory firm based in Sacramento, CA. We handle tax, fractional CFO, and back-office advisory for clients nationwide.



Pure tax preparation. 1040s with complexity, 1120-S, and 1065. Multi-state a plus. Must be able to own a return start to finish — not data entry support.



ProConnect Tax required. TaxSlayer, Drake, Lacerte experience considered but ProConnect is our platform — must learn it fast.



3+ years preparing returns at a tax firm or public accounting firm. Self-taught or corporate-only backgrounds are not a fit.



Part-time — tax season has wrapped but we have a significant return backlog to work through. If you're the right fit, this becomes a long-term relationship with a seat at the table next season.



Must be able to work some California business hours.

title: Personal Assistant to Director (MSP / IT Industry) – Remote | Part-time | Work from Home
description:
## Part-Time Personal Assistant to Directors (MSP / IT Industry) – Remote  

**Tel IT Solutions | Part-time | Work from Home | Adelaide / South Australia business hours (ACST/ACDT)**



Tel IT Solutions is a managed IT services and cybersecurity provider supporting Australian businesses. We’re looking for a highly organised **Part-Time Personal Assistant to the Directors** who understands the **MSP/IT services environment** and can help keep communications, admin, and operational follow-through running smoothly.



This role suits someone who has worked in the IT industry (ideally within an MSP) and is comfortable with MSP terminology, ticket/project workflows, and the pace of client-driven work.



### Key responsibilities

**Director support & communications**

- Manage director inboxes: triage, prioritise, draft replies, follow up, and keep communication moving

- Coordinate internal/external communications with clients, vendors, and tea
mbers

- Prepare meeting agendas, take notes/action items, and ensure follow-ups are completed



**Admin, operations & coordination**

- Assist with day-to-day admin and operational coordination across support and project work

- Help maintain internal documents, templates, registers, and basic reporting (as required)

- Liaise with suppliers for quotes, orders, renewals, and service documentation (where applicable)



**Accounts & business admin support**

- Support basic accounts/admin tasks such as invoice follow-ups, purchase/admin coordination, and document handling (exact scope can be agreed)

- Maintain organised records and ensure key documents are easy to find and up to date



**Documents, proposals & presentations**

- Create and format **draft proposals, contracts, SOWs, service documents, and client-ready communications**

- Build and polish **PowerPoint presentations** (sales decks, project updates, executive summaries)

- Assist with preparing quotes and proposal packs using existing templates and director input



### What we’re looking for (must-have)

- Experience working in the **IT industry**, ideally within an **MSP**

- Strong understanding of MSP terminology and workflows (tickets, SLAs, escalations, projects, change requests, onboarding/offboarding, renewals)

- Excellent written English and professional communication skills (you can draft
s that sound polished and clear)

- Strong skills in:

  - **Microsoft PowerPoint** (presentation building, formatting, layout consistency)

  - **Microsoft Excel** (spreadsheets, tracking, basic formulas, reporting)

  - **Microsoft Word** (formatting contracts/proposals, templates, version control)

- High attention to detail, confidentiality, and ability to manage competing priorities

- Self-starter who can work independently and proactively from home



### Nice-to-have (highly regarded)

- Experience drafting or coordinating **MSP proposals, service agreements, SOWs, and project documentation**

- Familiarity with Microsoft 365 admin concepts (helpful for terminology/context, not a technical role)

- Basic finance/admin exposure (invoicing processes, reconciliations support, vendor management)

- Experience with PSA/RMM tools (e.g., Atera, Halo, Autotask, ConnectWise, etc.)

- Light process improvement/documentation mindset (SOPs, checklists, templates)



### Role details

- **Part-time** (hours to be agreed; ideal for someone who can be available consistently during business hours)

- **Remote / Work from Home**

- Must be able to work aligned to **Adelaide / South Australia time (ACST/ACDT)**

- Immediate start preferred (if applicable)



### How to apply

Please send:

- Your CV, and

- A short note outlining:

  1) Your MSP/IT industry experience  

  2) Examples of proposals/presentations/contracts you’ve supported (no confidential content needed—just describe)  

  3) Your weekly availability (days/hours) in Adelaide time

title: Junior Virtual Assistant — Revenue Operations
description:
POSITION: Junior Virtual Assistant — Revenue Operations

COMPANY: 
 — AI-powered revenue recovery for home services businesses (HVAC, plumbing, electrical) across the United States

LOCATION: Remote (Philippines-based preferred)

REPORTING TO: Senior Virtual Assistant, with direct oversight from Business Development/Sales Lead

START DATE: April 27, 2026 (non-negotiable — we are in active pre-launch)



HOURS & SCHEDULE:

15-20 hours/week starting, scaling to 30 hours within 30 days based on VA performance and volume. Must be available during US Central Time business hours (8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CT) for a minimum of 4 consecutive hours daily. 

Preferred overlap: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM CT 



COMPENSATION: $5.00/hour USD, paid biweekly via Wise or PayPal. Performance review and rate adjustment at 30 and 90 days.



ABOUT REVSCOPE:

RevScope helps HVAC and home services companies recover revenue from their existing customer databases — lapsed customers, unconverted quotes, and dropped leads. We use AI-powered outreach combined with human follow-up to book appointments directly on the business owner's calendar. We are launching our Founding Member program on May 4, 2026, and need immediate execution support.



WHAT YOU WILL DO EVERY DAY:




 Lead Research & Data Management (40% of time):

Pull targeted prospect lists from 
 using filters: HVAC / Plumbing / Electrical industry, $1M–$10M revenue, owner or general manager title, Texas + neighboring states (AZ, FL, OK, LA, GA, TN, NC, SC, AL). Export lists including: company name, owner/decision-maker name, title,
 address, direct phone number, company phone, city, state, estimated revenue, employee count. Clean every list before import: remove duplicates against master list, verify
 deliverability using Apollo's built-in verification, flag entries missing phone numbers or
s for manual research, cross-reference against Google Maps to confirm the business is operational (active website, recent reviews, listed phone number). Maintain a master prospect spreadsheet in Google Sheets with standardized columns and zero duplicates. Daily target: 40–60 new verified, enriched contacts ready for import.



GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM Operations (30% of time):

Import cleaned contact lists into GHL with correct tags: vertical (HVAC, plumbing, electrical), geography (state, city), source (Apollo, referral, inbound), quality tier (A/B/C based on revenue size and data completeness), and pipeline stage. Maintain pipeline accuracy: update every contact's status within 24 hours of any interaction 
 opened, replied, call scheduled, call completed, enrolled, declined). Monitor all active GHL automations daily: check that
 sequences are firing on schedule, SMS workflows are triggering correctly, and no automations have errored or stalled. Flag any automation failures to Sr. VA immediately. Tag contacts who engage (open 2+
s, click a link, reply) as "Engaged" for priority follow-up. Tag contacts who reply negatively or unsubscribe as "Opted Out" and remove from active sequences. Prepare daily GHL pipeline report (Google Sheet or GHL dashboard screenshot): total contacts by stage, new leads added,
s sent/opened/replied, calls scheduled, enrollments.



Outreach Execution Support (20% of time):

Send personalized
 outreach using approved templates in GHL. Personalize the first line of every
 with one company-specific detail: Google review count and rating, years in business (from Google or website), specific services they promote, a recent positive or negative review quote. Track all outreach metrics in GHL and the daily report. 

Daily target: 30–50 personalized
s sent with less than 2% bounce rate.



Social Media & Administrative Support (10% of time):

Schedule pre-written social media posts using Buffer or native platform scheduling under supervision of Operations Manager. Monitor LinkedIn,
, and Instagram for inbound comments and DMs — screenshot and forward any inquiries to Business Development/Sales Lead within 1 hour. Research JV partner prospects (podcasters, newsletter writers, trade associations) and compile contact information. Maintain shared Google Drive with organized folders for SOPs, templates, reports, and meeting notes. Schedule calls and meetings on Business Development/Sales Lead's calendar when requested.



TOOLS YOU WILL USE DAILY:

GoHighLevel (GHL) — CRM, pipeline management,
/SMS automation. Training will be provided during onboarding week; candidates with existing GHL experience will be prioritized. 


 — lead research and prospecting. 

Google Workspace — Sheets, Docs, Drive, Calendar. 

Buffer or Hootsuite — social media scheduling. 

Slack or WhatsApp — daily communication with Sr. VA & Operations Manager and Business Development/Sales Lead. 

Loom — recording screen-share updates and questions.



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS:

Minimum 1 year in a virtual assistant, lead generation, data entry, or CRM management role. Proficient in Google Sheets including filtering, deduplication, VLOOKUP, and basic pivot tables. Strong written English —
s must be grammatically flawless and sound natural, not robotic. Reliable high-speed internet (minimum 10 Mbps) and quiet workspace with functioning webcam and headset. Self-directed — able to execute a daily task list with minimal supervision after initial training. 



Available to start by April 27, 2026.



PREFERRED (not required, but you move to the top of the list):

Experience with GoHighLevel. Experience with 
, 
, or similar B2B prospecting tools. Previous work supporting a US-based startup, marketing agency, or SaaS company. Familiarity with HVAC, plumbing, electrical, or home services industry. Comfort using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) as research and writing aids.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE AT 30 DAYS:

Apollo prospect lists for designated locations (we will provide these) are complete — 2,000+ verified contacts imported into GHL and segmented correctly. GHL pipeline is accurate and current, updated daily, with zero stale records. Outreach
s are going out at 30–50/day with consistent personalization quality and sub-2% bounce rate. Daily pipeline report is delivered to Business Development/Sales Lead by 12:00 PM CT every business day without being asked. Business Development/Sales Lead and Operations Manager spend zero time on data cleaning, contact imports, or routine outreach sending.



TO APPLY:

Record a 2–3 minute video introduction in English. Show your face. Tell me: 

(1) your relevant experience, 

(2) which CRM platforms you've used, 

(3) why you want this role, and 

(4) when you can start. 

Include a screenshot or screen recording (client names redacted is fine) of any CRM dashboard, spreadsheet, or data management work you've done. Provide 2 references with contact information. Send everything to 
 or application channel.

title: Human Resources (HR) Lead
description:
We are hiring a highly organized, detail-oriented Human Resources Lead to support a fast-growing, multi-location franchise education business in the U.S.



This is not a basic VA role — you will act as the operational backbone for HR, payroll coordination, onboarding, compliance, and related administrative systems across multiple locations.



Your job is to reduce chaos, streamline workflows, and ensure clean execution across HR as we scale.



Key Responsibilities



HR Operations & Employee Lifecycle

• Manage onboarding for new hires (offer letters, document collection, file organization)

• Maintain employee records and ensure all documentation is complete and compliant with California employment laws

• Support offboarding processes (terminations, final pay coordination, documentation)

• Track employee status across locations (active, onboarding, terminated)



Payroll Coordination (Paylocity)

• Prepare and coordinate payroll runs

• Handle off-cycle payroll adjustments, corrections, and updates

• Maintain employee data (direct deposit, classifications, etc.)

• Work with U.S. payroll provider (Paylocity) to resolve issues



Systems & Benefits Administration

• Support Paylocity setup and ongoing maintenance (T&L, policies, configurations)

• Assist with benefits coordination (401k, benefit platforms, etc.)

• Troubleshoot system issues and follow up persistently until resolved

• Help document and improve HR/payroll workflows



Compliance & Entity Setup Support

• Assist with: EIN applications, State registrations (e.g., EDD), Business licenses

• Maintain compliance tracking (deadlines, filings, documentation)

• Conduct basic research on HR/payroll/legal requirements as needed



Admin & Operations Support

• Manage shared tools (Google Drive, tracking sheets, task lists)

• Coordinate across tea
mbers (operations, finance, leadership)

• Reduce
 chaos by organizing and executing tasks efficiently

• Ad-hoc personal requests from owner



Reporting & Process Improvement

• Maintain clean tracking sheets for: Payroll status, Onboarding progress, Compliance items

• Identify inefficiencies and propose improvements

• Help build simple, scalable SOPs for all recurring tasks



Ideal Candidate Profile

• 3–6+ years experience working in Human Resources roles.

• Experience working with U.S.-based businesses (required), with experience in California-based businesses a significant nice-to-have

• Familiarity with: Payroll systems (Paylocity strongly preferred); HR processes (onboarding, terminations, employee records)

• Highly organized with strong attention to detail

• Strong written English communication (clear, concise, professional)

• Able to work independently and drive tasks to completion without handholding



Nice to Have

• Experience supporting multi-location or franchise businesses

• Familiarity with tools like: Paylocity, Ramp, Google Sheets (intermediate level)

• Experience improving workflows or building SOPs

• Basic knowledge of U.S. labor/payroll compliance



Success Metrics

• Payroll runs smoothly with minimal errors or rework

• Employee onboarding/offboarding is clean, fast, and fully documented

• Minimal back-and-forth required to complete tasks

• Systems (Paylocity, tracking sheets) are clean and up-to-date

• Communication is clear, proactive, and reduces leadership time



Time Tracking

• You will be asked to maintain a simple Google Sheet time tracker that tracks your time spent by project

title: Editor Quality Checker
description:
Position: Part-Time Video Quality Checker (QC Editor)

Company: Kan Social



We’re looking for a detail-oriented male candidate (18–25 years old) with past video editing experience to join our team as a Video Quality Checker. This is a part-time role (1–2 hours per day) with clear opportunities for growth and promotion inside the company. You MUST be able to speak very good and clear english. You must also watch a lot of anime, american shows, podcasts, 
s, and about games (overwatch, rdr2, valorant, apex legends etc) or at least do enough research to understand it. Over time you will also be instructed to do more stuff and have a chance to be paid more. I'm simply looking for a loyal quality checker. 



What you’ll do:



Quality check short-form 



Review videos for pacing, cuts, captions, framing, audio sync, and overall quality



Follow a clear QC checklist and company standards



Leave clear notes and feedback for editors



Help ensure every video meets Kan Social’s quality bar before delivery



Requirements:



Past video editing experience (Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or similar)

Strong attention to detail

Fast learner and open to training

Reliable and consistent



Able to be active during U.S. hours (9 AM – 5 PM Pacific Time) for communication and availability



Comfortable working independently



Schedule & Pay:



1–2 hours per day to start



Part-time with potential for more hours



Opportunity to take on more responsibilities over time (reviews, operations support, higher-level QC)



Promotion path available for strong performers



Why this role is different:

This is not just a checker role. If you perform well, you’ll be trained further and can move up into higher responsibility positions within Kan Social.



To apply, send:

A short introduction about yourself

Your editing experience

Any past work or portfolio (if available)

And a voice note or a video of you speaking

mbers.

THIS IS NOT FOR PEOPLE THAT JUST STARTED OR PEOPLE WHO ARE JACK OF ALL TRADES. WE NEED A PERFECTIONIST - A SUBLIMATION EXPERT IN SIZING GRADING AND NESTING using latest software or scripts.



We are an International, Multi-Million-Dollar Manufacturing Company, that Specializes in providing High End printed materials and Super High End Sublimated Apparel to companies across the US and several countries. We are the Largest Full-service Printing Company in our area and our Dye Sublimation Cut & Sew Apparel Division is growing like wildfire.



We are looking for a few Talented people who have multiple skill sets. A few highly qualified applicants who can do the job requirements above. 



This is FULL TIME Only. No part time positions available.



Must have at least 5 years experience and be exceptional with whatever script or software you use to grade/size designs for production and nest for layout. 



What we are offering:

Full time work - This is permanent position offer of FULL TIME 40 Hour Work, per week.

We need several people asap. We are crazy overloaded. We need people who can do it all. Fast. Effective and on point.



Job Type: Full-time

Expected hours: 40 per week



Schedule:

8 hour shift

Monday to Friday 9-6 EST ( NEW YORK TIME )

, web-form, phone) and qualify prospects for production of apparel/signage/graphics.

 and communication skills; ability to build relationships virtually.

Include your preference on compensation structure.

title: Social Media Management
description:
About Breaklabs



Breaklabs is a growing sports card breaking channel on Whatnot running live rips of NBA, NFL, MLB, and Pokémon products. We're obsessed with running clean, hype-driven shows — great camera work, fair break formats, and real relationships with our regulars. We're looking for a social media manager who lives and breathes the hobby to help us turn Breaklabs into a destination channel.



The Role



You'll own everything that happens outside the live show — the content that gets people to click "Join Show" and the community that keeps them coming back. This is a hands-on creator/marketer role, not a scheduling job. If you can't talk comfortably about PSA pop reports, Bowman 1sts, Prizm color tiers, or National Treasures RPAs, this probably isn't the right fit.



What You'll Do



Run our Instagram, TikTok, and YT Shorts accounts — posting daily, engaging in the replies, and building a recognizable voice for the channel

Cut show footage into short-form content (pulls, reactions, funny moments, customer hits) and get it posted within hours of the break, not days

Design graphics and break announcements in Canva/Figma — show schedules, featured product drops, giveaway posts, winner callouts

Write captions that actually sound like a breaker wrote them, not a brand

Grow our follower base on each platform with clear weekly targets

Manage giveaways and community engagement plays that drive viewers into live Whatnot shows

Track which content drives the most Whatnot follows and show attendance, then double down

Coordinate with the on-camera team on show schedule, featured breaks, and promotional pushes



What You Bring



Deep familiarity with the sports card hobby — you know the products, the rip culture, and the personalities. Whatnot watching history is a big plus

Proven experience managing social media for a creator, brand, or breaker (send us the handles)

Strong video editing chops in CapCut, Premiere, or similar — fast turnaround matters more than cinematic polish

Graphic design ability in Canva or Figma

A good ear for short-form hooks and an eye for pull moments worth clipping

Self-directed — you'll get direction, not hand-holding



Nice to Have



Experience running social for another breaker or card shop

ManyChat, Meta Ads, or paid social experience

Your own collection (show it off in your application)

title: Customer Service Specialist e-com $100K+/month Experience Required
description:
We’re looking for a customer service specialist who has worked with e-commerce stores doing at least $100K/month.



You understand that customer service is not just answering
s it’s directly tied to revenue, refunds, and brand perception.



We run a fast-growing fashion store (US market), and we need someone who can handle volume while keeping the experience calm, professional, and conversion-focused.



Your Responsibilities



* Respond to customer
s (Gmail / helpdesk tools)

* Handle inquiries about orders, shipping, sizing, returns

* Manage refunds, complaints, and chargeback-sensitive situations

* Turn frustrated customers into satisfied ones

* Spot patterns (e.g. sizing issues, product complaints) and report them

* Help reduce refunds and increase customer satisfaction



What We’re Looking For



You have:



* Experience with e-commerce stores doing $100K+/month

* Strong written English (natural, human — not robotic)

* Ability to stay calm under pressure and high volume

* Understanding of customer psychology

* Experience with tools like Shopify, Zendesk, Gorgias or similar



IMPORTANT – Application Requirement



To apply, include:



* Stores you worked for 

* Estimated monthly revenue

* Your exact responsibilities

* How many tickets/emails you handled per day

* One example of how you handled a difficult customer



Applications without this will be ignored.



What Makes You a Good Fit



* You don’t just “reply” — you solve problems

* You understand how to protect the brand while keeping customers happy

* You can recognize when a refund is needed vs when to push back



We prefer candidates who have worked in fast-scaling stores and understand the pressure of volume and expectations.





regards, benjamin

title: Social Media & Sales Virtual Assistant
description:
**Position: Social Media & Sales Virtual Assistant (Remote – Philippines Preferred)**



We are a fast-growing coaching and real estate brand helping women rebuild their credit, finances, and become homeowners after divorce.



We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant who can help manage social media, engage with leads, and support daily business operations.



---



**Responsibilities:**



* Respond to comments and DMs on TikTok & Instagram

* Engage with followers and trigger conversations (lead generation)

* Organize and track leads daily

* Post and schedule content

* Assist with basic Canva designs (templates provided)

* Upload content to platforms (TikTok, IG, YouTube Shorts)

* Help manage
 or text follow-ups (scripts provided)



---



**Requirements:**



* Strong written English (clear and conversational)

* Experience with social media (TikTok, Instagram)

* Comfortable talking to leads in DMs

* Organized and able to follow instructions

* Familiar with Canva (or willing to learn quickly)

* Reliable internet and availability daily



---



**Bonus (Not Required but Preferred):**



* Experience in real estate or credit repair

* Experience using Beacons, Stan Store, or CRM systems

* Sales or lead conversion experience



---



**Hours & Pay:**



* Part-time to start (4–6 hours/day)

* Opportunity to grow to full-time

* Competitive pay based on experience

* Performance bonuses available based on results



---



**How to Apply:**



Please send:



1. A short introduction about yourself

2. Your experience with social media or VA work

3. A sample message you would send to someone who comments “REBUILD” on a post about credit/homeownership

4. Your availability (hours per day)



---



We are looking for someone long-term who wants to grow with the brand.

title: Social Media Content Creator — Real Estate Photography (Reels, Posts & Stories)
description:
I'm a real estate photographer and videographer based in Miami, FL offering photography, drone footage, video, 3D tours, floor plans, and more. I'm looking for a creative, detail-oriented social media content creator to help me grow my Instagram and
 presence consistently.

This is a content creation role only — you will NOT need access to my social accounts. All finished content will be delivered to me and I will handle posting myself.



What You'll Do:



Edit short-form Reels (15–20 seconds) from photos and video clips I provide, using popular and trending Instagram audio

Design Instagram feed posts and carousels using my media

Create Stories graphics and templates

Write captions with relevant, trending hashtags for every piece of content

Deliver all content on schedule every week without reminders

Maintain a consistent, polished, high-end aesthetic that fits a luxury real estate media brand



What I'll Provide:



All photos, video clips, and drone footage

Brand colors and style references

Weekly or bi-weekly batches of fresh media to work from

Clear feedback and direction as needed



Required Skills:



Strong video editing in CapCut — this is a non-negotiable requirement

Canva or Adobe for graphics and carousel/posts/stories design

A sharp eye for aesthetics — clean, modern, luxury look

Proven experience creating Reels and short-form content with captions that follow current trends

Good written English for captions and daily communication

Reliable, deadline-driven, and proactively communicates any issues



Bonus Skills (not required but a strong plus):



Experience editing professional RAW real estate video in DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro, or similar software

Familiarity with real estate, architectural, or interior photography content



Weekly Deliverables:



3 Reels (15–20 sec each, with trending IG audio)

4 Carousels (feed posts)

5 Stories

Captions + hashtags for everything



Pay — Per Deliverable:



Reels: $4 each

Carousels: $3 each

Stories: $2 each

Weekly total: ~$34/week ($136/month)



Payment is sent weekly or bi-weekly upon delivery and your approval. One round of revisions is included per piece. This is part-time to start with strong potential to grow as the account grows.



Schedule:

Flexible hours, fully async. Weekly deadline is firm. Must be available for a short check-in via WhatsApp or
/Instagram message.



To Apply:

I will not review applications that do not include all three of the following:



2–3 samples of content you have personally created or edited — Reels, Stories, carousels, or feed posts (links to Instagram, YouTube, or Google Drive accepted)

A brief description of your experience with CapCut and any other tools you use

Confirmation that you accept the per-piece rate (~$34/week to start) and your available hours per week

title: Experienced B2B Cold Caller To Help Schedule Appointments
description:
** PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING **



Hey, I am JT! I am looking for 1-2 hard working appointment setters for my small business. I'm not a big call center where you are just a number. I'm a small business owner and looking for help doing outbound B2B cold calling for me.  I am looking to hire immediately.



I help small business owners with an A.I. Receptionist to answer missed calls and after hours calls. Right now every day I cold call myself and it's been a lot of fun, but as I grow I am looking to outsource this responsibility.



YOUR ROLE:

-- Source your own leads, leads are not provided.

-- Call 80+ small business owners and offer them a free consultation to learn about A.I.

-- Schedule them for a free consultation if they seem interested.

-- That's it! Once they show-up to their appointment, I take over.



REQUIREMENTS (DO NOT APPLY UNLESS YOU MEET ALL THE CRITERIA):

-- Experienced B2B Cold Calling and Appointment Setting. This means 100% cold calling — no prior contact, no warm leads.

-- If you have only worked inbound leads, warm leads, or marketing leads, DO NOT APPLY.

-- No heavy accent.

-- You are not someone who relies on your hourly wage, but someone who is motivated by performance and earning more through high commissions.

-- You are a hard worker. You do not call out sick, make excuses, or show-up late.



PAYMENT:

-- $4/hour base 

-- $20 each qualified appointment that shows up

-- 1st monthly sale = $100

-- 2nd monthly sale = $150   ($250 total)

-- 3rd monthly sale = $300   ($550 total)

-- 4th monthly sale = $350    ($900 total)

-- 5th monthly sale = $500    ($1,400 total)



MATH EXAMPLE (At Only 3 Sold + 12 Show-Ups)

-- $320: 20 hours/week at $4/hour

-- $240: 12 show-ups 

-- $550: 3 sold 

-- Total: $1,110/month

-- Most new reps aim for 2-3 per month - that's where you can do very well here.



HOW TO APPLY:

-- Please do not send a generic message or one written with chat GPT - those will be ignored.

-- Do not mention previous jobs that are not specifically related to cold calling B2B.

-- Put "I Love To Dial" in your subject line

-- Copy and paste the following questions and put answers in your application:



1) How many months/years of experience do you have specifically cold calling B2B and appointment setting (only put jobs that were entirely cold leads....no warm leads, no inbound calls, etc):

2) What product/service were you offering and to who (please be detailed):

3) How many calls per day did you average:

4) How many appointments per week did you average:

5) Did you source your own leads or were you given leads:

6) Do you currently have another job:

7) What is more important to you (a base hourly to be safe, or huge commissions to earn even more money):

title: Creative Strategist | Meta Ads
description:
We’re a fast-growing 7-figure DTC brand looking for a full-time Creative Strategist to take full ownership of our ad creative direction.



This is not a “come up with a few ideas” role.



This is a high-output, high-learning, high-ownership role where you’ll drive 15–20 new ad concepts per week and work directly with the founder to scale winning creatives inside a live Meta ad environment.



If you want to grow with a real brand (not just freelance random projects), this is for you.



???? What You’ll Own



Develop 15–20 new ad concepts weekly



Turn data + learnings into iteration plans



Analyze winners & losers inside Meta



Build structured creative testing roadmaps



Write hooks, angles, scripts, and briefs



Work closely with our video editor



Lead weekly learnings calls (collaborative, not reporting)



You are not just ideating.

You are driving creative strategy that directly impacts revenue.



???? Our Environment



7-figure DTC brand



Daily ad spend



Structured CBO testing



Rapid feedback loops



Clear KPIs



We test aggressively and iterate fast



We believe:



No “random testing”



No ego on concepts



Data > opinions



Learnings compound weekly



Our learnings calls are collaborative.

We review performance together, break down direction, and d
 next moves as a team.



???? What Success Looks Like



Strong concept hit rate



Clear documentation of learnings



Faster iteration cycles



Scaling winners



Improving CPA & creative efficiency over time



???? Who This Is For



You’ve worked with DTC brands before



You understand Meta creative testing



You can explain why an ad won (not just that it did)



You can create structure from chaos



You want long-term growth, not gig work



You want to rise with a growing brand



???? Not For You If



You need heavy micromanagement



You struggle with high output



You don’t enjoy performance pressure



You’ve never managed creative in a paid ads environment



???? Role Details



(20 hrs/week) - Subject to change



Long-term position



Competitive salary (based on experience)



Performance bonus structure available for scaled winners



???? To Apply



Send:



Portfolio (ads you worked on)



The best performing ad you helped scale (include spend + CPA/ROAS)



How many concepts per week you’ve handled before



A short Loom explaining your creative testing process



Subject line:

“Creative Strategist – I Drive Winners”

title: Customer Service Representative
description:
Role Overview:



We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Customer Service Officer to support debt collection and

follow-up activities for the Australian market. This role is primarily
-based, with outbound calls

required when necessary. The ideal candidate has strong written communication skills, can respond

promptly, and is comfortable liaising with multiple stakeholders including customers, insurance

companies, and solicitors.



Duties and Responsibilities:

? Primarily manage customer and stakeholder communication through
, ensuring timely and professional responses.

? Conduct outbound calls when necessary to follow up on pending matters.

? Handle debt collection follow-ups, including tracking outstanding payments and coordinating resolutions.

? Liaise with customers, insurance companies, and other relevant parties to obtain updates, documents, and payment statuses.

? Coordinate with solicitors when needed and seek guidance on specific cases or instructions.

? Monitor and track all cases, ensuring follow-ups are completed within expected timelines.

? Maintain accurate and detailed records of all communications, updates, and outcomes in the system.

? Escalate delays, issues, or complex cases to the appropriate team or supervisor.

? Strong written English communication skills with the ability to draft clear, professional, and concise
s.

? High responsiveness and ability to manage multiple follow-ups efficiently.

required).

title: Senior Accountant (Real Estate)
description:
Hiring: Senior Accountant - Short-Term Rentals



We’re hiring a full-time Senior Accountant with deep, hands-on experience in short-term rental accounting (Airbnb, VRBO).



Key Responsibilities:

• Reconcile Airbnb & VRBO payouts to PMS and bank deposits (Stripe/PayPal)

• Record and audit gross bookings, host fees, cleaning fees, and platform commissions

• Handle deferred revenue, accruals, and revenue recognition per stay period

• Manage owner statements, payouts, and trust/liability accounts

• Perform monthly close, including journal entries, balance sheet reconciliations, and variance analysis

• Track and reconcile occupancy taxes, remittances, and platform-collected taxes

• Maintain clean chart of accounts aligned with STR operations

• Support financial reporting and property-level profitability analysis



Requirements:

• Proven experience in short-term rental / property management accounting (Airbnb, VRBO required)

• Strong proficiency in Sage Intacct or QuickBooks Online

• Experience with PMS systems (Guesty, Hostaway, AppFolio, etc.)

• Solid understanding of multi-entity, trust accounting, and OTA integrations

• Advanced Excel skills (recs, tie-outs, large data sets)



Schedule: Full-time, PST hours



Please do NOT apply if you have no experience in short-term rentals.



Send a link to your resume and specific STR experience.



What We Offer

• HMO coverage

• 13th month pay

• Paid holidays

• Paid time off (PTO)

• Remote setup

title: Part Time Zapier Automation & Real Estate Sales and Marketing Assistant - 20260421
description:
Must answer all questions in job description. Candidate who do not read through details and apply without ansewr to the evaluation questions will not be contacted.



About the Role

I am a realtor and interior designer based in Canada seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Remote Real Estate Virtual Assistant to support daily operations. This role is ideal for someone experienced in zapier automation with customization and some custom code editing, real estate research, data organization, scheduling, and marketing support. You will work closely with me to help streamline operations, transactions, research properties, and maintain marketing activities.



Key Responsibilities



1. Simple Web and Automation Support

- Experience in updating Wordpress websites

- Experience in setting up task automation usng Zapier or Maker



2. Property Research

- Search for properties based on specific client criteria using MLS and other real estate platforms

- Compile property lists for buyers and investment opportunities

- Monitor new listings and notify the realtor of relevant opportunities



3. Price & Market Analysis

- Conduct competitive market analysis (CMA) on comparable properties

- Research recent sales, active listings, and neighborhood trends

- Prepare organized reports and spreadsheets summarizing market data



4. Appointment Coordination

- Schedule property showings, meetings, and client appointments

- Coordinate calendars and confirm appointments with clients and vendors

- Send reminders and maintain organized schedules



5. Marketing Support

- Assist with social media posting and online marketing campaigns

- Prepare listing marketing materials (flyers, brochures,
 campaigns)

- Support CRM updates and lead tracking

- Help organize open house marketing and follow-ups



6. Administrative Support

- Maintain property and client databases

- Organize documents and files related to listings and transactions

- Assist with basic
 communication and task coordination



Qualifications

- Previous experience as a Real Estate Virtual Assistant is a must; not just property management

- Familiarity with Zapier a must

- Strong research and data organization skills

- Familiarity with real estate terminology and basic market analysis

- Experience with spreadsheets (Google Sheets or Excel)

- Experience with CRM systems and marketing tools is a plus

- Strong written English communication skills

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and reliable

- Ability to work independently and meet deadlines but also able to collaborate in team environment and do zoo
etings daily



Preferred Skills

- Experience with web develop tools and process automation tools a must

- Experience working with North American real estate agents preferred

- Knowledge of MLS systems, Zillow, or similar platforms

- Graphic design experience (Canva or similar tools)

- Familiarity of AI assisted content generation: writing, images, and video

- Social media management experience (Instagram,
, LinkedIn)



Compensation

- fixed pay; will not consider hourly

- Long-term opportunity and potentially convert to full time if interested



How to Apply, Please submit:

1. Are you able to work 4 hours a day / 5 days a week Sunday to Thursday PST during PST office hours?

2. Your resume

3. A short introduction about your experience working on Zapier

4. A short introduction about your experience working with real estate agents

5. Portfolio examples of any automation, real estate, marketing work, administrative support, website work you have done

6. Your expected monthly pay for part time 4 hours a day / 5 days a week role
